docs.title: Getting Started with MessWala
docs.text: Welcome to MessWala! This guide will help you get up and running in minutes.

docs.title: 🎯 What is MessWala?
docs.text:
MessWala is a comprehensive mess management system designed for hostels and shared living spaces. It helps manage:

  • Expenses — Track shared costs and calculate fair distribution
  • Meal Attendance — Mark daily meal participation
  • Menu Planning — Organize and communicate meal schedules
  • Feedback — Rate meals and provide suggestions
  • Analytics — View spending trends and participation metrics

docs.title: I'm an Admin (Setting up a new mess)
docs.text:
1. Deployment Guide — Deploy the application
  2. Admin Setup Wizard — Configure your mess
  3. User Management — Approve initial users

docs.title: I'm a Manager/Treasurer
docs.text:
1. Getting Started — Login to your account
  2. Features Guide — Learn about expense tracking & analytics
  3. API Reference (if integrating) — Connect to MessWala

docs.title: I'm a Student/Resident
docs.text:
1. Getting Started — Login to your account
  2. Features Guide — Mark attendance, view menu, give feedback
  3. User Roles — Understand your permissions

docs.title: Step 1: Access MessWala
docs.text: Live URL: https://mess-walah.vercel.app

docs.title: Option A: Google Sign-In (Recommended)
docs.text:
1. Click "Login with Google" 2. Select your Google account 3. Authorize MessWala 4. System auto-creates your account 5. Complete your profile if first login

Advantages: - ✅ No password to remember - ✅ Secure (Google-backed) - ✅ Fast login - ✅ Works on mobile

docs.title: Option B: Email & Password
docs.text:
1. Click "Don't have an account? Register" 2. Enter your email address 3. Create a strong password 4. Fill in your details: - Full Name - College ID - Room Number - Phone Number 5. Submit for approval

After registration: - Admins receive approval notification - Your account is pending until approved - You can login once approved

docs.title: Step 3: Complete Your Profile
docs.text: On first login, update: - Profile Picture (optional) - Contact Information (phone, email) - Additional Details (room, preferences)

docs.title: 🎨 Dashboard Overview
docs.text: After login, you'll see your personalized dashboard with:

docs.title: Dashboard Cards
docs.text:
• Mess Information — Quick stats about your mess
  • Expense Summary — Monthly spending overview
  • Attendance Rate — Your meal participation percentage
  • Quick Actions — Shortcuts to common tasks

docs.title: Navigation Menu
docs.text: 📊 Dashboard (Home & overview) 💰 Expenses (Track & view expenses) ✋ Attendance (Mark meal attendance) 🍽️ Menu (View meal schedule) 💬 Feedback (Rate meals) 📈 Analytics (View reports & trends) ⚙️ Settings (Manage account)

docs.title: Empty States
docs.text:
When you first login to a new mess, some sections show "No data yet": - Expenses — Managers haven't recorded any yet - Attendance — No meals marked - Menu — No meals planned

This is normal. Data populates as managers and members interact with MessWala.

docs.title: As a Manager/Treasurer
docs.text:
Recording an Expense: 1. Go to Expenses page 2. Click [Add Expense] 3. Select Category (Vegetables, Rice, Gas, etc.) 4. Enter Amount 5. Add Description (optional) 6. Set Date (defaults to today) 7. Click [Save]

Viewing Expenses: - See all expenses in a table - Filter by category or date range - View monthly trends in chart - Download CSV reports

docs.title: As a Student
docs.text: Viewing Expenses: - See all mess expenses - Understand fair cost distribution - Cannot add expenses (unless manager role) - Your "fair share" calculated automatically

docs.title: How Attendance Works
docs.text:
Marking Attendance for Today's Meals: 1. Go to Attendance page 2. See meal cards (Breakfast, Lunch, Dinner) 3. Click [Mark Present] if you ate that meal 4. Click [Mark Absent] if you skipped

Attendance History: - View past week's attendance - See who attended each meal - Track your own monthly participation

docs.title: Why Attendance Matters
docs.text:
• Fair Expense Sharing — Only active members share costs
  • Meal Planning — Managers forecast portions based on attendance
  • Analytics — Identify popular and unpopular meals

docs.title: Viewing Menu
docs.text: 1. Go to Menu page 2. See this week's schedule 3. View meals by day and type (Breakfast/Lunch/Dinner) 4. Read dish names and notes

docs.title: As a Manager: Planning Meals
docs.text: 1. Go to Menu page 2. Click [Edit Menu] (if manager) 3. Select day and meal type 4. Enter dishes (comma-separated) 5. Add notes (optional) 6. Save

docs.title: Giving Feedback
docs.text: 1. Go to Feedback page 2. Select a meal and date 3. Rate from 1-5 stars (1=Poor, 5=Excellent) 4. Add optional comments 5. Submit

docs.title: Why Feedback Matters
docs.text:
• Managers see which meals need improvement
  • Identify dietary preferences
  • Track satisfaction trends

docs.title: Expense Analytics
docs.text:
• Monthly breakdown with charts
  • Category distribution (pie chart)
  • Spending trends over time
  • Per-person fair share calculation

docs.title: Attendance Analytics
docs.text:

  • Member participation trends

docs.title: Health Score

docs.title: Available Options
docs.text:
Profile Settings: - Update full name - Change profile picture - Update contact information - Change password

Preferences: - Email notifications - Language selection - Dashboard layout

Security: - Change password - Linked accounts (Google) - Session management - Login history

docs.title: 🔐 Security Tips
docs.text:
Do: - ✅ Use a strong, unique password - ✅ Enable 2FA on Google account - ✅ Log out on shared computers - ✅ Report suspicious activity

Don't: - ❌ Share your password - ❌ Use the same password as other accounts - ❌ Click links in suspicious emails - ❌ Login on unsecured WiFi without VPN

docs.title: ❓ Common Questions
docs.text:
Q: How do I reset my password? A: Click "Forgot Password" on login page. Check your email for reset link.

Q: Can I change my role? A: Only admins can change roles. Contact your mess admin.

Q: Where's my expense report? A: Go to Analytics > Expenses. Export as CSV.

Q: Why can't I see Expenses page? A: Your role might not have permission. Ask admin.

Q: How is fair share calculated? A: Fair Share = Total Expenses ÷ Active Members Active = attended at least 1 meal this month.

Q: Can I access offline? A: No, MessWala requires internet. Data syncs automatically.

docs.title: Login Issues
docs.text:
"Invalid credentials" - Double-check email and password - Reset password if forgotten - Try Google Sign-In instead

"User not approved" - Registration pending admin approval - Ask admin to check pending registrations - Typically approved within 24 hours

"Changes not saving" - Check internet connection - Verify message says "Saved successfully" - Try again after 5 seconds - Contact admin if persists

docs.title: Performance Issues
docs.text: "Page loading slowly" - Check internet speed - Close other browser tabs - Try different browser - Clear cache and cookies

docs.title: Twilio WhatsApp + Gmail Setup Guide
docs.text: This guide helps you set up WhatsApp notifications via Twilio with email (Gmail) as a fallback for the MessWala hostel management system.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The notification system uses: - Primary Channel: Twilio WhatsApp (when configured) - Fallback Channel: Gmail/SMTP (automatic fallback if WhatsApp fails) - User Choice: Users can select their preferred channel: email, whatsapp, or both

docs.title: Part 1: Gmail Setup (Email Notifications)
docs.text: Gmail is used as the fallback and email notification channel.

docs.title: Step 1: Enable 2-Factor Authentication
docs.text:
1. Go to myaccount.google.com
  2. Click Security in the left sidebar
  3. Enable 2-Step Verification if not already enabled
  4. Follow Google's prompts to verify your phone number

docs.title: Step 2: Generate App Password
docs.text:
1. Go to myaccount.google.com/apppasswords
  2. Select:
  3. App: Mail
  4. Device: Windows/Mac/Linux (whichever applies)
  5. Click Generate
  6. Google will display a 16-character password
  7. Copy this password (you'll use it in .env)

docs.title: Step 3: Update Environment Variables
docs.text:
Add these to your .env file:

```env

docs.title: Gmail Configuration
docs.text: SMTP_EMAIL=your-email@gmail.com SMTP_PASSWORD=xxxx xxxx xxxx xxxx # Your 16-character app password ```

docs.title: Step 4: Test Gmail
docs.text:
Run: bash npm run health:check

Check the backend logs for "Email sent" messages, or use the test endpoint:

bash curl -X POST http://localhost:5000/api/notifications/test \ -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_JWT_TOKEN" \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -d '{"channel": "email"}'

docs.title: Part 2: Twilio WhatsApp Setup
docs.text: Twilio provides WhatsApp integration with both a free trial and production options.

docs.title: Step 1: Create a Twilio Account
docs.text:
1. Go to twilio.com
  2. Click Sign up (free trial available)
  3. Complete the account verification
  4. Accept the Developer Terms

docs.title: Step 2: Get Twilio Credentials
docs.text:
1. Visit Twilio Console
  2. Your Account SID and Auth Token are displayed on the main page
  3. Copy these values (keep Auth Token private!)

docs.title: Step 3: Get a WhatsApp-Enabled Twilio Phone Number
docs.text:
Option A: Use Twilio WhatsApp Sandbox (Free Trial)

  1. Go to Twilio WhatsApp Sandbox
  2. You'll see a sandbox phone number like: Sandbox Number: +14155238886
  3. The sandbox is pre-configured for development

Option B: Get Production WhatsApp Number

  1. In Twilio Console, go to Phone Numbers → Manage Numbers
  2. Click Get started or Get a phone number
  3. Search for numbers in your country
  4. Verify it supports WhatsApp
  5. Purchase the number

docs.title: Step 4: Add Test Phone Number to Sandbox
docs.text:
For Twilio Sandbox Only (Development)

  1. Go to WhatsApp Sandbox
  2. Scroll to "Sandbox Participants"

  3. Send a message from your personal WhatsApp to the sandbox number: join <unique-code> (The unique code is shown in the Sandbox page, e.g., join flying-ocean)

  4. You'll receive a confirmation. You're now registered!

docs.title: Step 5: Update Environment Variables
docs.text:
Add these to your .env file:

```env

docs.title: Twilio WhatsApp Configuration
docs.text: TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID=ACxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN=your-auth-token-here TWILIO_PHONE_NUMBER=+14155238886

docs.title: Your test phone number (for sandbox development)
docs.text:
TWILIO_TEST_PHONE=+91xxxxxxxxxx ```

For Production (After Moving Off Sandbox)

Remove TWILIO_TEST_PHONE and ensure TWILIO_PHONE_NUMBER is your purchased production number.

docs.title: Step 6: Update User Phone Numbers
docs.text:
Users must provide phone numbers in their profiles for WhatsApp:

Frontend Form: javascript // User must input phone in format: +91-XXXXXXXXXX or 10-digit number // System automatically formats to E.164 format (+91XXXXXXXXXX)

API Endpoint: bash curl -X POST http://localhost:5000/api/users/profile \ -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_JWT_TOKEN" \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -d '{"phone": "+919876543210"}'

docs.title: 1. Check Configuration
docs.text:
bash curl http://localhost:5000/api/notifications/config/check \ -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_JWT_TOKEN"

Expected Response: json { "success": true, "config": { "gmail": { "configured": true, "email": "your***" }, "twilio": { "configured": true, "accountSid": "ACxx***", "phoneNumber": "+14155238886", "testPhoneConfigured": true }, "whatsappSupported": true } }

docs.title: 2. Send Test Notification
docs.text:
Test email channel: bash curl -X POST http://localhost:5000/api/notifications/test \ -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_JWT_TOKEN" \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -d '{"channel": "email"}'

Test WhatsApp channel: bash curl -X POST http://localhost:5000/api/notifications/test \ -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_JWT_TOKEN" \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -d '{"channel": "whatsapp"}'

Test both channels (with automatic fallback): bash curl -X POST http://localhost:5000/api/notifications/test \ -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_JWT_TOKEN" \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -d '{"channel": "both"}'

docs.title: 3. Expected Behavior
docs.text:
Email Channel: - ✅ You receive an email at SMTP_EMAIL address - ✅ Email contains formatted HTML template with MessWala styling

WhatsApp Channel: - ✅ You receive a WhatsApp message at your configured phone - ✅ Message format: "Title\n\nContent" - ⚠️ If WhatsApp fails (e.g., disabled account), falls back to email - ✅ Fallback is logged in console

Both Channel (Fallback): - ✅ Tries WhatsApp first - ✅ If WhatsApp fails, automatically sends via email - ✅ Check logs for fallback messages: [warn] Failed to send whatsapp notification [info] Primary channel failed. Attempting fallback... [info] Fallback: Email sent successfully after WhatsApp failure

docs.title: Issue: "Twilio not configured. WhatsApp notifications disabled."
docs.text:
Solution: Check environment variables bash echo $TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID echo $TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN

Ensure both are set in .env or system environment.

docs.title: Issue: WhatsApp Message Not Delivered (Sandbox)
docs.text: Solutions: 1. Not registered: Send join <code> to sandbox number again 2. Sandbox expired: Sandbox numbers reset every 72 hours of inactivity 3. Phone number not formateed: Use format +91XXXXXXXXXX for India (no hyphens)

docs.title: Issue: "User phone number not available"
docs.text: Solution: User must add phone number to profile first bash curl -X POST http://localhost:5000/api/users/profile \ -H "Authorization: Bearer TOKEN" \ -d '{"phone": "+919876543210"}'

docs.title: Issue: Gmail App Password Not Working
docs.text: Solutions: 1. Ensure 2FA is enabled on Gmail account 2. Use the 16-character password (without spaces) or xxxx xxxx xxxx xxxx format 3. Check password is copied correctly (no extra spaces) 4. Try regenerating the App Password in Google Account settings

docs.title: Issue: "WhatsApp: The number is not registered"
docs.text:
For Sandbox: Register your phone again with join command

For Production: Ensure: 1. Phone number is verified in Twilio 2. Phone is active and can receive WhatsApp 3. Number format is E.164 (+ followed by country code)

docs.title: Fallback Mechanism (Automatic)
docs.text:
The system automatically falls back to email if WhatsApp fails:

javascript // When channel = 'both': 1. Try sending via WhatsApp 2. If WhatsApp fails: - Log warning about WhatsApp failure - Attempt email as fallback - Log success of fallback 3. Status: 'sent' if any channel succeeded 4. Notification includes: fallbackUsed: true

Use Cases: - WhatsApp account disabled → Falls back to email ✅ - Phone number invalid → Falls back to email ✅ - Twilio rate limited → Falls back to email ✅ - Email disabled → Only WhatsApp is attempted

docs.title: Before Going Live:
docs.text:
1. Move Off Sandbox:
  2. Purchase a verified WhatsApp number in Twilio Console
  3. Remove TWILIO_TEST_PHONE from .env

  4. Update TWILIO_PHONE_NUMBER to your production number

  5. WhatsApp Business Account (Required for production):

  6. Verify your business
  7. Use WhatsApp Business API instead of sandbox

  8. Configure message templates for compliance

  9. Set Environment: env NODE_ENV=production

  10. Rate Limiting:

  11. Twilio free trial: 100 messages/month
  12. Production: Pay per message (~$0.08 per message)

  13. Implement rate limiting in NotificationScheduler

  14. Testing in Production: bash npm run health:check # View logs, ensure no errors

docs.title: Check Configuration
docs.text:
``` GET /api/notifications/config/check Headers: Authorization: Bearer

Response: { "success": true, "config": { "gmail": { "configured": true, ... }, "twilio": { "configured": true, ... }, "whatsappSupported": true } } ```

docs.title: Send Test Notification
docs.text:
``` POST /api/notifications/test Headers: Authorization: Bearer Body: { "channel": "email|whatsapp|both" }

Response: { "success": true, "message": "Test notification sent successfully", "details": { "email": "sent", "whatsapp": "sent" }, "fallbackUsed": false } ```

docs.title: Get Preferences
docs.text: GET /api/notifications/preferences Response: { "success": true, "channel": "both", "preferences": { "mealReminders": true, ... }, "phoneConfigured": true }

docs.title: Update Channel Preference
docs.text: POST /api/notifications/preferences/channel Body: { "preferredNotificationChannel": "email|whatsapp|both" }

docs.title: Notification Types
docs.text:
Supported notification types with their channels:

Type Email WhatsApp Use Case MEAL_REMINDER ✅ ✅ Daily meal attendance reminder PAYMENT_DUE ✅ ✅ Payment deadline notification COST_SUMMARY ✅ ❌ Monthly cost breakdown DISCREPANCY_ALERT ✅ ✅ Attendance mismatch warning MEAL_UPDATE ✅ ❌ Meal count changes EXPENSE_ADDED ✅ ❌ New expense in system BUDGET_EXCEEDED ✅ ✅ Budget limit alert ATTENDANCE_CONFIRMATION ✅ ✅ Confirm attendance request

docs.title: User Roles & Permissions
docs.text: MessWala has three user roles, each with specific permissions and responsibilities. Understanding your role is key to using MessWala effectively.

docs.title: What is an Admin?
docs.text: Admins are system administrators responsible for: - Initial mess setup and configuration - User approval and management - System maintenance - Full access to all features

docs.title: Permissions
docs.text: Feature Permission Details Dashboard Full View all analytics & user activity Expenses View & Edit Can view all, add new, edit/delete own Expenses Analytics Full Monthly trends, category breakdown, per-person costs Attendance View See all member attendance Attendance Analytics Full Patterns, rates, member details Menu View & Edit Can create, update, delete menus Feedback View See all feedback & ratings User Management Full Approve, reject, edit, assign roles Mess Configuration Full Set categories, meal times, mess info System Settings Full Database, security, integrations Reports Full Export any data as CSV/PDF

docs.title: First-Time Setup (Admin Only)
docs.text:
When an admin logs in for the first time, they must:

  1. Complete Setup Wizard:
  2. Mess name and description
  3. Contact information
  4. Expense categories

  5. Meal time schedule

  6. Verify Database Connection

  7. Check MongoDB connectivity

  8. Verify configuration stored

  9. Approve Initial Users

  10. Review pending registrations
  11. Accept or reject members
  12. Assign roles as needed

docs.title: Daily Tasks
docs.text:
• ✅ Approve pending registrations
  • ✅ Review expense submissions (optional)
  • ✅ Monitor system health
  • ✅ Respond to user issues

docs.title: Weekly Tasks
docs.text:
• ✅ Review analytics reports
  • ✅ Check attendance trends
  • ✅ Approve expense reports
  • ✅ Plan upcoming menus

docs.title: Monthly Tasks
docs.text:
• ✅ Generate financial reports
  • ✅ Audit fair share calculations
  • ✅ Review feedback trends
  • ✅ Planning next month

docs.title: What is a Manager?
docs.text: Managers handle financial and operational aspects: - Record and categorize expenses - Create and manage menus - View analytics and reports - Support admin in day-to-day operations

docs.title: Permissions
docs.text: Feature Permission Details Dashboard Limited View summaries & trends Expenses Full Add, edit, view all, delete own Expenses Analytics Full All trends, reports, per-person costs Attendance View See attendance records Attendance Analytics Full View patterns & detailed reports Menu View & Edit Create, update, delete menus Feedback View See all feedback User Management View List all users, see status Mess Configuration Edit Cannot change (admin only) System Settings None No access Reports Full Export expense & attendance data

docs.title: Daily Tasks
docs.text:
• ✅ Record expenses (grocery, utilities, etc.)
  • ✅ Categorize expenses properly
  • ✅ Update menu if needed
  • ✅ Monitor feedback

docs.title: Weekly Tasks
docs.text:
• ✅ Review expense trends
  • ✅ Plan next week's menu
  • ✅ Check attendance rates
  • ✅ Communicate with members

docs.title: Monthly Tasks
docs.text:
• ✅ Generate expense report
  • ✅ Calculate fair share per member
  • ✅ Review attendance summary
  • ✅ Plan next month's budget

docs.title: Manager Key Features
docs.text:
Expense Tracking: Go to Expenses page ├─ Add new expense ├─ Choose category ├─ Enter amount & description ├─ View all expenses ├─ Filter by date/category └─ Export reports

Menu Planning: Go to Menu page ├─ View current month ├─ Click Edit Menu ├─ Select day & meal time ├─ Enter dishes ├─ Add notes (spicy level, allergies, etc.) └─ Save schedule

Analytics: Go to Analytics page ├─ Expense breakdown (pie chart) ├─ Monthly trends (line chart) ├─ Fair share calculation ├─ Attendance rates └─ Member participation

docs.title: What is a Student?
docs.text: Students (residents/members) are: - Individual mess members - Mark their meal attendance - Provide feedback on meals - View shared information

docs.title: Permissions
docs.text: Feature Permission Details Dashboard Limited Personal overview only Expenses View View all expenses, your fair share Expenses Analytics View Monthly breakdown, trends Attendance Full Mark own, view past Attendance Analytics View View own attendance %, trends Menu View View upcoming meals Feedback Full Submit ratings & comments User Management None No access Mess Configuration None No access System Settings None No access Reports None No access

docs.title: Daily Tasks
docs.text:
• ✅ Mark attendance for meals eaten
  • ✅ View today's menu
  • ✅ Check your fair share amount

docs.title: Weekly Tasks
docs.text:
• ✅ Review attendance for the week
  • ✅ Provide feedback on meals
  • ✅ Check upcoming menu

docs.title: Monthly Tasks
docs.text:
• ✅ Review your fair share calculation
  • ✅ Pay your portion to appropriate person
  • ✅ Review attendance rate

docs.title: Student Key Features
docs.text:
Marking Attendance: Go to Attendance page ├─ See today's meals: │ ├─ Breakfast (7:30 - 9:00 AM) │ ├─ Lunch (12:30 - 2:00 PM) │ └─ Dinner (7:30 - 9:00 PM) ├─ Click Mark Present (if you ate) ├─ Click Mark Absent (if you didn't) └─ View past attendance

Giving Feedback: Go to Feedback page ├─ Select meal & date ├─ Rate 1-5 stars ├─ Add comments (optional): │ ├─ Taste │ ├─ Quantity │ ├─ Suggestions │ └─ Allergen warnings └─ Submit

Viewing Menu: Go to Menu page ├─ See this week's schedule ├─ Check meal times ├─ Read dishes & descriptions └─ Plan your participation

Understanding Fair Share: Your cost = (Total expenses ÷ members who attended) × your attendance

Example: - Total expenses: ₹3000 - Members attended at least 1 meal: 10 - Your attendance: 7 out of 10 meals

Your share: (3000 ÷ 10) × 7 = ₹2100

docs.title: How to Get a Different Role
docs.text:
Student → Manager: - Ask admins for role upgrade - Admins review your participation - Admins confirm responsibility - Your role changes immediately

Current Manager → Student: - Admins can downgrade if needed - You lose manager permissions - Your data remains accessible - Clear handoff recommended

Any Role → Admin: - Requires explicit admin action - Not usually granted to students - New admins need training - Discuss before granting

docs.title: Requesting Role Change
docs.text:
1. Contact your mess admin
  2. Explain why you need the upgrade
  3. Discuss responsibilities
  4. Admin approves and updates

docs.title: Admin-Level Security
docs.text:
• Full audit log access
  • Cannot be removed by other users
  • All actions logged
  • Special authentication required

docs.title: Manager-Level Security
docs.text:
• Expense verification enabled
  • Actions logged for audit
  • Cannot change own role
  • Permissions reviewed monthly

docs.title: Student-Level Security
docs.text:
• Personal data encrypted
  • Can only edit own records
  • View-only access to shared data
  • No system settings access

docs.title: For Admins
docs.text: When assigning roles, ask: - ✓ Does this person understand responsibilities? - ✓ Do they have the time commitment? - ✓ Are they trustworthy with financial data? - ✓ Have they been formally chosen by the mess? - ✓ Do others agree with the choice?

docs.title: For Managers
docs.text: Before accepting, confirm: - ✓ You understand expense tracking system - ✓ You can commit 1-2 hours weekly - ✓ You're comfortable with key-keeping/finances - ✓ You have backup support

docs.title: ❓ Common Questions
docs.text:
Q: Can a student view other students' attendance? A: No. Students see only overall stats, not individual records.

Q: Can a manager approve users? A: No. Only admins can approve registrations.

Q: What if admin isn't available? A: Appoint a backup admin. Multiple admins can exist.

Q: Can I have multiple roles? A: No. Each user has one primary role.

Q: Do roles expire? A: No, unless admins change them.
